stories.title: Victor Marx wins Colorado GOP primary for governor
stories.description: The Marine veteran and ministry founder will face Attorney General Phil Weiser in the November general election.

stories.title: China, Russia, Iran amplify US backlash to AI data centers
stories.description: State media outlets exploited local concerns over energy costs and environmental impacts to undermine American AI infrastructure, according to new reports.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says China warned Russia against nuclear use
stories.description: The Ukrainian president said Beijing issued an "ultimatum"-style warning to Moscow, marking what he called China's firmest stance since the invasion began.

stories.title: Israel warned U.S. of fresh Iranian plot to kill Trump
stories.description: The intelligence emerged as U.S.-Iran hostilities escalated sharply, with both sides trading strikes after Trump declared an eight-week ceasefire over.

stories.title: Kirk suspect wept and expressed regret, roommate tells court
stories.description: Lance Twiggs testified that Tyler Robinson confessed to killing Charlie Kirk two days after the shooting, then cried and said he wished he hadn't done it.

stories.title: Mexico to file criminal complaints in US over ICE custody deaths
stories.description: Foreign Minister Roberto Velasco said 14 Mexican nationals have died in ICE custody this year, with three more killed during arrest operations.

stories.title: Poorly worded clause in Trump's Iran deal fuels Hormuz crisis
stories.description: A dispute over Paragraph 5 of the U.S.-Iran agreement has triggered repeated confrontations at the strait, with nearly 6,000 seafarers stranded.

stories.title: Maine Democrats approve 600-delegate convention to replace Platner in Senate race
stories.description: The unprecedented nominating convention follows Platner's withdrawal from the race against Sen. Susan Collins after a sexual assault allegation.

stories.title: US hits Iranian rail bridge as peace deal unravels
stories.description: IRGC says cruise missiles struck a Belt and Road rail corridor in Golestan province, vowing a "crushing response" after overnight strikes killed 14.

stories.title: Meta to begin producing in-house AI chip 'Iris' in September
stories.description: An internal memo shows the company plans to manufacture its "Iris" data center chip as it aims to double computing capacity to 14 gigawatts by 2027.

stories.title: Platner suspends Maine Senate bid amid assault allegation
stories.description: The Democratic nominee's exit triggers a scramble to pick a replacement candidate to challenge Sen. Susan Collins by July 27.

stories.title: Millions gather as Khamenei funeral continues in Iraq
stories.description: Massive processions filled Najaf and Karbala on Wednesday as Trump declared the U.S.-Iran ceasefire "over" and signaled further military action.

stories.title: Idaho mother who blamed vaccines for twins' deaths charged with murder
stories.description: Andrea Shaw, lead plaintiff in a Children's Health Defense lawsuit, was indicted on two counts of first-degree murder after a grand jury found she suffocated her toddlers.

stories.title: Former judge who helped immigrant evade ICE spared prison, fined $5K
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Lynn Adelman cited Hannah Dugan's law-abiding life, rejecting prosecutors' push for 15 to 21 months behind bars.

stories.title: Trump offers Ukraine Patriot license, backs strikes on Russia
stories.description: At the NATO summit in Ankara, Trump endorsed Ukraine's deep strikes on Russian refineries and offered a license to produce Patriot missiles.

stories.title: China plans to let top AI firms buy Nvidia H200 chips
stories.description: Beijing is moving to end a monthslong deadlock that left U.S.-approved chip exports frozen due to conflicting Chinese and American use requirements.

stories.title: Blue Origin raising $10B in first outside funding round
stories.description: The rocket company, funded almost entirely by Jeff Bezos for 26 years, is valued at $130 billion in the deal led by Coatue Management.

stories.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to rehear birthright citizenship case
stories.description: The president called the court's 6-3 ruling striking down his executive order "a miscarriage of justice" and demanded immediate reconsideration.

stories.title: Former Pfizer building in Manhattan deemed stable after collapse scare
stories.description: Most evacuated residents have been cleared to return as the restricted zone shrinks and an investigation into the structural failure continues.

stories.title: ICE agent fatally shoots man during Houston operation
stories.description: The FBI is investigating after an ICE officer killed Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican national who ICE alleges rammed an agency vehicle.

stories.title: SSN glitch delays some parents from opening Trump Accounts for newborns
stories.description: The Treasury Department says a verification issue is blocking some 2026 babies with newly issued Social Security numbers from enrollment.

stories.title: Le Pen says she will run for France’s presidency in 2027
stories.description: A Paris appeals court upheld her embezzlement conviction but reduced her office ban, making her immediately eligible to run next April.

stories.title: Lawmakers to press Bessent on Russia sanctions at NATO summit
stories.description: A bipartisan congressional delegation plans to confront the Treasury secretary at a Tuesday dinner in Ankara over the stalled Sanctioning Russia Act.

stories.title: Judge orders Trump to pay E. Jean Carroll $5.8M
stories.description: A federal judge authorized the release of funds held in escrow, rejecting a last-minute filing by Trump's attorneys to block the payout.

stories.title: Ukraine finds Monaco bombing suspect shot dead near Kyiv
stories.description: A military intelligence officer confessed to killing Anastasiia Berezovska, the Interpol-wanted suspect in the June 29 attack on a Ukrainian tycoon.

stories.title: US trade deficit jumps 42% in May on record imports
stories.description: Capital goods imports hit a record $128 billion, driven by semiconductors and computer accessories tied to AI data center buildout.

stories.title: Trump says he will remove Syria from terrorism list
stories.description: The announcement came during a bilateral meeting with Syrian President al-Sharaa at the NATO summit in Ankara.

stories.title: Belgium routs USMNT 4-1 to end US World Cup run
stories.description: Charles De Ketelaere scored twice as Belgium eliminated the United States in the Round of 16 on Monday in Seattle.

stories.title: CISA now using Anthropic's Mythos to audit government code
stories.description: The deployment caps months of effort to get the powerful AI vulnerability-detection model into the hands of the lead U.S. civilian cybersecurity agency.

stories.title: Trump declares Iran ceasefire 'over,' threatens to cut trade with Spain at NATO summit
stories.description: As U.S. and Iranian forces traded strikes overnight, the president told allies in Ankara the preliminary truce was finished and singled out Spain for punishment.

stories.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, offers power to UN-backed panel
stories.description: Israel dismissed the move as "deception with no practical significance," saying Hamas members remained in their positions.

stories.title: Preliminary hearing begins for man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
stories.description: Prosecutors in Provo are presenting evidence against Tyler Robinson as Kirk's widow and parents face the defendant for the first time.

stories.title: Hegseth cancels Israel visit as US-Iran strikes escalate
stories.description: The defense secretary's trip was meant to address Israeli concerns over Trump's openness to selling F-35 jets to Turkey.

stories.title: Iran's hardliners demand revenge as Khamenei funeral procession moves through Tehran
stories.description: Tehran's airspace was shut down Monday as millions joined the procession and military commanders vowed to avenge the slain leader.

stories.title: Three Khamenei sons appear at funeral; successor stays hidden
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei remains absent from his father's massive state funeral over fears Israel could assassinate him.

stories.title: FEMA threatens to withhold terrorism funds over election rules
stories.description: States must overhaul voting systems or lose 20% of homeland security grants under new conditions issued by the Trump administration.

stories.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed at Madison Square Garden
stories.description: Adam Sandler officiated as roughly 1,000 guests watched the couple marry Friday night in custom Dior at the iconic NYC arena.

stories.title: White supremacist group Patriot Front marches through DC on America's 250th birthday
stories.description: Hundreds of masked white nationalists carried Confederate flags and chanted anti-immigrant slogans near the Capitol on Saturday amid July 4th celebrations.

stories.title: Housing bill set to become law as Trump's deadline to act expires
stories.description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act will take effect without the president's signature after he refused to sign the landmark legislation.

stories.title: US-Iran nuclear talks to resume July 11 in Pakistan
stories.description: Negotiations paused for Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral will resume with sanctions, frozen assets, and Tehran's nuclear program on the agenda.

stories.title: Messi scores 20th World Cup goal as Argentina edges Cape Verde 3-2 in extra time
stories.description: Defending champions needed extra time to survive Cape Verde in a round of 32 thriller at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens.

stories.title: NATO launches $40B counter-drone push at Ankara summit
stories.description: Allies announced the Drone Edge initiative and tens of billions in arms deals as leaders seek to show Trump that defense pledges are becoming real capabilities.

stories.title: Houthis threaten Saudi airports over Yemeni airspace dispute
stories.description: The warning came after Saudi fighter jets allegedly tried to stop an Iranian civilian plane from landing at Sanaa airport.

stories.title: Fired Joint Chiefs chair CQ Brown warns against domestic troop use
stories.description: In a Foreign Affairs essay published ahead of July Fourth, Brown and co-authors say politically charged military missions erode combat readiness and public trust.

stories.title: Trump says Greenland should be under US control at NATO summit
stories.description: Speaking alongside Turkey's Erdogan in Ankara, the president said the territory "doesn't help Denmark" and should belong to America.

stories.title: France pulls aircraft carrier from Gulf, citing US-Iran deal progress
stories.description: Macron ordered the Charles de Gaulle back to Toulon but kept mine-clearance vessels and frigates near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Brussels rejects UK bid for decision-making role in EU
stories.description: The dispute over governance in post-Brexit trade talks has further delayed a reset deal already stalled by Labour's leadership transition.

stories.title: Trump admin keeps same firm to repair Reflecting Pool liner
stories.description: The Interior secretary confirmed on Sunday the administration will not seek new bids, keeping the original contractor despite ongoing scrutiny of no-bid deals.

stories.title: June heatwave may have killed 20,000 in Europe, study estimates
stories.description: Swiss glaciers hit their annual tipping point weeks early as researchers warn the historic late-June heat dome exposed $15.6 trillion in economic activity to climate-amplified conditions.

stories.title: Russia's fuel crisis spreads as gasoline runs out in major port city
stories.description: Novorossiysk reported zero gasoline at all stations on Thursday as Ukrainian drone strikes knock out a third of Russia's refining capacity.

stories.title: Trump delivers late-night July 4 speech after storms delay America 250 celebration
stories.description: Severe weather forced a two-hour evacuation of the National Mall, pushing the president's remarks past 11 p.m. on the nation's 250th birthday.

stories.title: WHO declares cruise ship hantavirus outbreak over
stories.description: The two-month outbreak aboard the MV Hondius infected 13 people, killed three, and triggered monitoring across 33 countries.

stories.title: FBI orders 260 analysts to Atlanta for 2020 election probe
stories.description: Director Kash Patel's memo directs all field offices to "immediately surge support" to investigate the 2020 vote in Fulton County, Georgia.

stories.title: Trump pardons 11, including 9 for Clean Air Act violations
stories.description: Nine of the 11 were convicted of disabling or bypassing vehicle emissions controls, which Trump called wrongful prosecution for "fixing their car."

stories.title: Ex-Olympian indicted on felony charge over Reflecting Pool incident
stories.description: David Hearn, who says he only touched a loose piece of liner during a bike ride, now faces up to 10 years in prison.

stories.title: Security guard pulled alive 8 days after Venezuela quakes
stories.description: Hernán Alberto Gil Flores was extracted from a collapsed shopping center basement in La Guaira after surviving in an air pocket since June 24.

stories.title: Netanyahu, Trump agree to meet in US amid Iran deal tensions
stories.description: The Friday phone call came as reports alleged Israel plotted to assassinate Iranian negotiators during U.S.-Tehran talks this spring.

stories.title: Police fire tear gas as Albania 'Flamingo Revolution' protest turns violent
stories.description: Clashes outside parliament in Tirana injured 12 officers and led to 18 detentions as "Flamingo Revolution" protesters demanded PM Rama's resignation.

stories.title: Anthropic in talks with Samsung to build custom AI chip
stories.description: The discussions focus on Samsung's 2nm process technology, though no detailed design or manufacturing work has begun, according to The Information.

stories.title: Acting intel chief Pulte fires dozens more intelligence officials
stories.description: A second wave of terminations targeted those the Trump administration considers "deep state," according to an intelligence official who spoke anonymously.

stories.title: Iran rejects IAEA access to bombed nuclear sites after Doha talks
stories.description: Parliament Speaker Qalibaf said inspectors are limited to two facilities, as indirect U.S.-Iran talks in Doha yielded working groups but no breakthrough.

stories.title: US adds just 57,000 jobs in June, missing forecasts
stories.description: The sharp slowdown from spring's strong hiring streak raises fresh questions about labor market health amid elevated inflation.

stories.title: Trump returns to Mount Rushmore as White House backs adding his face
stories.description: The president is delivering a keynote address Friday at the monument's first fireworks display in six years, kicking off America's 250th birthday weekend.

stories.title: Vatican declares SSPX in schism, excommunicates all its clergy
stories.description: The decree goes beyond the six bishops involved in Wednesday's unauthorized consecration, extending sanctions to all SSPX priests and adherent laity.

stories.title: Driver charged with manslaughter in fatal Tesla crash into Texas home
stories.description: Michael Butler faces manslaughter charges after his Tesla Model 3 crashed into a Katy, Texas, home on June 19, killing a 76-year-old woman inside.

stories.title: Trump privately weighing clemency for Diddy Combs, CBS News reports
stories.description: The president is meeting Friday to discuss pardons ahead of the Fourth of July, with Combs among those under consideration.

stories.title: CSIS: Ukraine war casualties top 2 million since 2022
stories.description: A new study estimates Russia has suffered 1.4 million casualties, while Ukraine's toll ranges between 525,000 and 625,000.

stories.title: Musk denies report that SpaceX showed AI device to investors
stories.description: The Wall Street Journal reported SpaceX presented a slim, AI-powered handset prototype to investors ahead of its IPO, but Musk called the claim "utterly false."

stories.title: Trump says US destroyed Iran's radar in three nights of strikes
stories.description: In a CNBC interview, Trump detailed how American forces hit Iranian radar systems and escorted oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Two masked climbers scale Empire State Building spire, unfurl peace banner
stories.description: One climber appeared to propose to the other on a spire platform before the pair descended as NYPD helicopters hovered nearby.

stories.title: Russia shuts rail border crossings with Finland, Estonia, Latvia
stories.description: Moscow suspended all rail traffic through seven crossings effective Wednesday, offering no explanation or end date for the closures.

stories.title: Hegseth's  Europe troop cuts blocked by Rubio ahead of NATO summit
stories.description: The White House pulled back further force reductions after internal pushback, shifting focus to defense deals at next week's Ankara summit.

stories.title: Meta building cloud business to sell excess AI compute
stories.description: The social media giant plans to sell access to AI models and raw computing capacity, challenging AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.

stories.title: Iran says nuclear talks won't advance until Lebanon commitments are met
stories.description: Chief negotiator Ghalibaf called enrichment a "non-negotiable" red line as Netanyahu vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon indefinitely.

stories.title: Trump made 21,000 stock trades in 2025, disclosure shows
stories.description: The 927-page filing reveals hundreds of purchases the day before his tariff pause triggered a historic market rally.

stories.title: US lifts export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 AI model
stories.description: The Commerce Department's move completes the reversal of restrictions that forced Anthropic to take its most advanced AI models offline for nearly three weeks.

stories.title: House GOP rebels tank defense bill vote over SAVE Act demands
stories.description: Fourteen Republicans voted down a procedural rule for the $1.15 trillion NDAA, freezing the House floor days before July 4 recess.

stories.title: Tucker Carlson vows to build new third party after breaking with Trump
stories.description: Carlson told the Columbia Journalism Review he hasn't spoken to Trump since the Iran war began and called the U.S. "a one-party state."

stories.title: USDA plans $500M aid for small meatpackers amid cattle shortage
stories.description: The funding excludes the four largest beef processors and aims to keep smaller slaughterhouses operating as losses mount to $300 per head.

stories.title: Supreme Court agrees to decide if AR-15 bans violate Second Amendment
stories.description: The justices granted review in cases from Illinois and Connecticut, setting up the most significant gun rights showdown since the 2022 Bruen decision.

stories.title: OpenAI proposes giving U.S. government a 5% stake, FT reports
stories.description: The offer, worth roughly $42.6 billion, is the most concrete step yet in months of talks between Sam Altman and Trump administration officials.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes Moscow satellite facility in 419-drone barrage
stories.description: Zelensky confirmed drones hit Russia's Dubna Space Communications Center again as a six-month-old baby was killed in the Moscow region.

stories.title: Rep. Tom Kean Jr. returns to Congress, reveals depression diagnosis
stories.description: The New Jersey Republican disclosed that depression was behind his nearly four-month absence, during which he missed more than 100 votes.

stories.title: US in advanced talks with AI firms on voluntary model release standards
stories.description: The discussions aim to operationalize Trump's June executive order, which created a framework for pre-release government review of frontier AI models.

stories.title: Supreme Court strikes down campaign spending limits
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling removes 1974 caps on coordinated spending between political parties and candidates ahead of the November midterms.

stories.title: UK signals intervention in Paramount's $110B Warner Bros. deal
stories.description: Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy said she is "minded to intervene" in the proposed merger, citing concerns about media plurality for British audiences.

stories.title: Trump reports $1.4B in crypto income as investors lost billions
stories.description: His 927-page financial disclosure shows total 2025 income of at least $2.2 billion, more than triple what he reported the year before.

stories.title: Supreme Court upholds state bans on transgender athletes
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling rejected equal protection and Title IX challenges, backing laws in West Virginia and Idaho that restrict team participation by biological sex.

stories.title: Russian recruits surviving only minutes at Ukraine's drone-dominated front
stories.description: New soldiers last 20 to 35 minutes in combat positions as drone warfare drives losses past 30,000 per month, according to a Foreign Policy essay.

stories.title: Trump renews vow to block China from Panama Canal at Roosevelt library dedication
stories.description: Speaking at the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library opening in North Dakota, Trump reprised his second-term push to counter Chinese influence over the waterway.

stories.title: Venezuela accused of blocking aid, rescuers after quakes kill over 1,700
stories.description: Critics say government restrictions on access to the hardest-hit areas are hindering rescue operations five days after twin earthquakes struck.

stories.title: San Francisco Archdiocese reaches $395M abuse settlement
stories.description: The agreement resolves more than 500 clergy sexual abuse claims filed under California's AB 218 and establishes a trust for survivors.

stories.title: Iran deal strains U.S.-Saudi ties as MBS hedges on Trump's diplomacy
stories.description: Gulf allies fear the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding grants Tehran dangerous leverage over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Europe heatwave kills over 1,300 as Paris morgues run out of space
stories.description: France alone has recorded 1,000 excess deaths since the heatwave began, overwhelming mortuaries and sparking political criticism of the government's response.

stories.title: JetBlue plane hits drone at 3,000 feet on approach to JFK
stories.description: The aircraft landed safely with no damage found, but the incident marks the second drone encounter near a New York-area airport in days.

stories.title: Trump takes first flight on Qatar-gifted Air Force One
stories.description: The president flew the converted Boeing 747-8 to North Dakota for the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library dedication.

stories.title: Supreme Court upholds state laws counting mail ballots after Election Day
stories.description: The 5-4 ruling in Watson v. RNC preserves grace periods in roughly 30 states ahead of the 2026 midterms.

stories.title: Comcast to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into separate company
stories.description: Shares surged as the company said it will split its media and entertainment assets from its broadband and technology operations within a year.

stories.title: Trump administration declines to extend USMCA trade pact
stories.description: The decision triggers annual reviews and a 10-year countdown that could see the North American free trade zone expire in 2036.

stories.title: Skydiving plane crash in France kills all 11 on board
stories.description: A Pilatus PC-6 carrying five skydiving students, five instructors, and a pilot crashed shortly after takeoff near Nancy on Sunday morning.

stories.title: South Korea scrambles jets as China, Russia hold air patrol
stories.description: Nearly 10 Chinese and Russian military aircraft briefly entered South Korea's air defense zone during Saturday's 11th joint strategic patrol.

stories.title: DOJ sues Virginia over assault weapons ban on day law takes effect
stories.description: The federal lawsuit challenges the state's ban on AR-15-style rifles as unconstitutional, extending the Trump administration's campaign against state gun laws.

stories.title: China strips generals, ex-regulator of lawmaker posts
stories.description: Xi Jinping's anti-corruption drive expands as the NPC removes eight senior officials, including six PLA generals and a Politburo member.

stories.title: Drone strikes Iranian Kurdish opposition camp near Erbil
stories.description: No casualties were reported after the camp was evacuated before Saturday's attack, the latest in a sustained campaign throughout 2026.

stories.title: Fed Chair Warsh vows to hit 2% inflation, rebuffs Trump on rates
stories.description: Speaking at the ECB's Sintra forum, Warsh said the Fed will remain independent and dismissed expectations of tolerating above-target inflation.

stories.title: Cape Verde becomes smallest nation to reach World Cup knockout round
stories.description: The tournament debutants finished second in Group H with three draws and will face defending champions Argentina on July 3 in Miami.

stories.title: Supreme Court calls Alito-Sotomayor clash a 'misunderstanding'
stories.description: The court said Friday that Alito was notified in advance of Sotomayor's oral dissent in a major asylum case but misunderstood the situation.

stories.title: Trump announces first-ever GOP midterm convention in Dallas
stories.description: The unprecedented Sept. 9-10 gathering aims to rally Republican voters ahead of competitive House and Senate races this November.

stories.title: CDC raises Ebola response to highest level amid DRC outbreak
stories.description: The Level 1 activation, the most severe designation, deploys maximum staff as the Bundibugyo strain spreads across central and east Africa.

stories.title: Texas approves Bible readings for 5M public school students
stories.description: The State Board of Education voted Friday to make Texas the first state to require Bible passages in its public school reading curriculum.

stories.title: Trump demands gas retailers cut prices to $2.50, warns of 'big problems'
stories.description: The president escalated his pressure campaign Monday, threatening retailers who don't lower pump prices despite a DOJ probe already targeting major oil companies.

stories.title: Israel, Lebanon reach framework deal after extended Washington talks
stories.description: The agreement establishes a mechanism for Lebanese army units to deploy into pilot zones vacated by Israeli forces in southern Lebanon.

stories.title: Mistrial declared in Palisades Fire arson trial
stories.description: Jurors voted 10-2 for acquittal but could not reach unanimity, forcing prosecutors to decide whether to retry the case.

stories.title: Supreme Court overturns 91-year-old limit on presidential firing power
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling in Trump v. Slaughter lets presidents dismiss leaders of independent agencies without cause, reshaping federal regulation.

stories.title: Newsom calls for national billionaires' tax while opposing California's version
stories.description: The governor proposed a minimum tax on those worth over $100 million and a public AI equity fund as he eyes a 2028 presidential run.

stories.title: Iran strikes caused $400M in damage to US Navy base, WSJ finds
stories.description: The Pentagon is now weighing relocating some Gulf installations to Israel and moving command centers underground, according to the investigation.

stories.title: Putin rejects Ukraine's ceasefire proposal, vows to seize four regions
stories.description: Ukraine's foreign minister declared the "Spirit of Anchorage" between Trump and Putin "certainly dead," urging Russia to negotiate directly.

stories.title: SpaceX plans Starlink mobile service to rival U.S. wireless carriers
stories.description: Gwynne Shotwell told IPO roadshow investors that SpaceX could sell mobile contracts directly to consumers and build its own terrestrial network.

stories.title: NYC approves historic rent freeze on stabilized apartments
stories.description: The Rent Guidelines Board voted Thursday to freeze rents on roughly one million apartments, delivering on Mayor Mamdani's central campaign promise.

stories.title: Trump declares reflecting pool 'in full use,' eyes D.C. golf course
stories.description: The president said construction on a public golf course near the National Mall will begin in September as part of his broader reshaping of the capital.

stories.title: NY prosecutors drop Weinstein rape charge after accuser declines to testify again
stories.description: Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's office dismissed the third-degree rape charge after Jessica Mann said she "can no longer go through this."

stories.title: US tests Ukrainian Magura naval drones in Philippines
stories.description: The combat-proven Magura V5 sank a target ship during Balikatan 2026, marking its first use outside the Russia-Ukraine war.

stories.title: Trump sons invested in Kazakhstan deal their father brokered, NYT finds
stories.description: A New York Times investigation reveals Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump took a stake in a $1.6 billion taxpayer-backed tungsten project weeks after the president helped negotiate it.

stories.title: Supreme Court rules federal law blocks Roundup cancer lawsuits
stories.description: The decision shields **Bayer** from thousands of claims alleging the company should have labeled the herbicide as a potential carcinogen.

stories.title: Macron says Europe and US in 'reconvergence' on Ukraine
stories.description: Europe's five biggest military powers met in Berlin on Wednesday to coordinate ahead of next month's NATO summit in Ankara.

stories.title: Trump taps Oklahoma trooper as ICE director nominee
stories.description: If confirmed, Lance Schroyer would be the first Senate-confirmed ICE director since 2017, ending nearly a decade of acting leaders.

stories.title: 6.9 magnitude earthquake strikes off northeastern Japan
stories.description: The quake hit near Kuji, Iwate Prefecture, on Thursday morning local time, prompting a tsunami warning for the region.

stories.title: ICC bureau recommends firing prosecutor Karim Khan
stories.description: Details of the June 8 decision emerged Tuesday, revealing findings of "non-consensual sexual contact" with a junior staff member.

stories.title: Trump threatens 100% tariff on countries with digital services taxes
stories.description: The sweeping threat, posted Friday on Truth Social, would override existing trade deals and target European nations considering taxes on U.S. tech giants.

stories.title: Rubio says U.S.-Iran talks to resume in Switzerland June 29
stories.description: The secretary of state, on a Gulf tour to reassure allies, also rejected any Iranian tolls on the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Venezuela set to reveal $240B debt in largest restructuring
stories.description: The figure far exceeds prior market estimates of $150 billion to $200 billion, according to the Financial Times.

stories.title: UN demands probes into 52 ICE custody deaths under Trump
stories.description: UN rights chief Volker Türk called for independent investigations as a new report found the ICE detention mortality rate has more than doubled.

stories.title: OpenAI unveils Jalapeño, its first custom AI chip
stories.description: Developed with Broadcom, the inference chip rivals Nvidia's Blackwell processors, according to Broadcom CEO Hock Tan.

stories.title: Ex-Adams chief of staff Frank Carone arrested by FBI
stories.description: Carone and three others face a federal bribery case tied to a city contract issued during the Adams administration.

stories.title: Trump admin seeks $1.4B for Ebola as Kenya facility faces scrutiny
stories.description: Public Health Service members describe rushed training for the controversial Kenya quarantine center as the outbreak nears 1,000 cases.

stories.title: China defends right to target people abroad under new ethnic unity law
stories.description: Vice Justice Minister Hu Weilie dismissed "long-arm jurisdiction" criticism, saying the law taking effect July 1 is "legitimate and lawful."

stories.title: US warns 'time is not on Moscow's side' as peace talks stall
stories.description: Putin and Lavrov insisted on Moscow's terms for Ukraine negotiations as the U.S. called for an immediate ceasefire at the UN Security Council.

stories.title: Judge lets lawsuit against Trump's $1.8B fund proceed, questions DOJ's 'trustworthiness'
stories.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday the DOJ's refusal to submit a sworn statement confirming the fund's closure keeps the legal challenge alive.

stories.title: Mamdani-backed candidates sweep three NYC congressional primaries
stories.description: The New York mayor's progressive slate toppled incumbents and establishment Democrats in Tuesday's primary races across the city.

stories.title: Kim commissions North Korea's largest warship, pledges nuclear navy
stories.description: The 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon entered service Tuesday after 14 months of testing, as Kim outlined plans for 10,000-ton warships.

stories.title: Supreme Court rules Trump can end TPS for Haitians, Syrians
stories.description: The 6-3 decision strips protections from over 350,000 immigrants and could affect up to 1.3 million TPS holders nationwide.

stories.title: Alan Wilson wins South Carolina GOP governor runoff
stories.description: Wilson defeated Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette by a wide margin after Trump hedged his endorsement by backing both candidates.

stories.title: Meta is building a prediction markets app to rival Polymarket and Kalshi
stories.description: CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed a team to develop "Arena," a standalone app that would initially use a points system instead of real money.

stories.title: Supreme Court lets Trump turn back asylum seekers at border
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling in Mullin v. Al Otro Lado holds that migrants standing in Mexico have not "arrived" in the U.S. under federal law.

stories.title: Bennett says Israel smuggled Starlink into Iran
stories.description: Former PM told a Jerusalem summit he initiated covert delivery of tens of thousands of satellite receivers to help Iranian protesters.

stories.title: DOJ charges 455 in $6.5 billion healthcare fraud takedown
stories.description: The annual crackdown includes a record number of Medicaid fraud defendants and targets schemes involving false billing, opioid distribution, and wound care fraud.

stories.title: Senate reverses Iran war powers vote after Trump confrontation
stories.description: Two Republican senators changed their positions late Wednesday after a shouting match between Trump and GOP lawmakers at the Capitol.

stories.title: DOJ, New York file dueling lawsuits over mask ban for federal agents
stories.description: New York's law barring officers from wearing face coverings takes effect Friday, setting up an urgent legal clash over immigration enforcement.

stories.title: US, Iran finalize deal to release $12B in frozen assets
stories.description: Iran's chief negotiator says the agreement, reached in Switzerland, will release the funds in two $6 billion tranches.

stories.title: Trump orders DOJ to probe Exxon, Chevron over gas prices
stories.description: The president accused major oil companies of "gouging" consumers by keeping pump prices high despite falling crude costs after the Iran peace deal.

top.title: Victor Marx wins Colorado GOP primary for governor
top.description: The Marine veteran and ministry founder will face Attorney General Phil Weiser in the November general election.

top.title: China, Russia, Iran amplify US backlash to AI data centers
top.description: State media outlets exploited local concerns over energy costs and environmental impacts to undermine American AI infrastructure, according to new reports.

top.title: Zelenskyy says China warned Russia against nuclear use
top.description: The Ukrainian president said Beijing issued an "ultimatum"-style warning to Moscow, marking what he called China's firmest stance since the invasion began.

top.title: Israel warned U.S. of fresh Iranian plot to kill Trump
top.description: The intelligence emerged as U.S.-Iran hostilities escalated sharply, with both sides trading strikes after Trump declared an eight-week ceasefire over.

top.title: Kirk suspect wept and expressed regret, roommate tells court
top.description: Lance Twiggs testified that Tyler Robinson confessed to killing Charlie Kirk two days after the shooting, then cried and said he wished he hadn't done it.

top.title: Mexico to file criminal complaints in US over ICE custody deaths
top.description: Foreign Minister Roberto Velasco said 14 Mexican nationals have died in ICE custody this year, with three more killed during arrest operations.

top.title: Poorly worded clause in Trump's Iran deal fuels Hormuz crisis
top.description: A dispute over Paragraph 5 of the U.S.-Iran agreement has triggered repeated confrontations at the strait, with nearly 6,000 seafarers stranded.

top.title: Maine Democrats approve 600-delegate convention to replace Platner in Senate race
top.description: The unprecedented nominating convention follows Platner's withdrawal from the race against Sen. Susan Collins after a sexual assault allegation.

top.title: US hits Iranian rail bridge as peace deal unravels
top.description: IRGC says cruise missiles struck a Belt and Road rail corridor in Golestan province, vowing a "crushing response" after overnight strikes killed 14.

top.title: Meta to begin producing in-house AI chip 'Iris' in September
top.description: An internal memo shows the company plans to manufacture its "Iris" data center chip as it aims to double computing capacity to 14 gigawatts by 2027.

top.title: Platner suspends Maine Senate bid amid assault allegation
top.description: The Democratic nominee's exit triggers a scramble to pick a replacement candidate to challenge Sen. Susan Collins by July 27.

top.title: Millions gather as Khamenei funeral continues in Iraq
top.description: Massive processions filled Najaf and Karbala on Wednesday as Trump declared the U.S.-Iran ceasefire "over" and signaled further military action.

top.title: Idaho mother who blamed vaccines for twins' deaths charged with murder
top.description: Andrea Shaw, lead plaintiff in a Children's Health Defense lawsuit, was indicted on two counts of first-degree murder after a grand jury found she suffocated her toddlers.

top.title: Former judge who helped immigrant evade ICE spared prison, fined $5K
top.description: U.S. District Judge Lynn Adelman cited Hannah Dugan's law-abiding life, rejecting prosecutors' push for 15 to 21 months behind bars.

top.title: Trump offers Ukraine Patriot license, backs strikes on Russia
top.description: At the NATO summit in Ankara, Trump endorsed Ukraine's deep strikes on Russian refineries and offered a license to produce Patriot missiles.

top.title: China plans to let top AI firms buy Nvidia H200 chips
top.description: Beijing is moving to end a monthslong deadlock that left U.S.-approved chip exports frozen due to conflicting Chinese and American use requirements.

top.title: Blue Origin raising $10B in first outside funding round
top.description: The rocket company, funded almost entirely by Jeff Bezos for 26 years, is valued at $130 billion in the deal led by Coatue Management.

top.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to rehear birthright citizenship case
top.description: The president called the court's 6-3 ruling striking down his executive order "a miscarriage of justice" and demanded immediate reconsideration.

top.title: Former Pfizer building in Manhattan deemed stable after collapse scare
top.description: Most evacuated residents have been cleared to return as the restricted zone shrinks and an investigation into the structural failure continues.

top.title: ICE agent fatally shoots man during Houston operation
top.description: The FBI is investigating after an ICE officer killed Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican national who ICE alleges rammed an agency vehicle.

top.title: SSN glitch delays some parents from opening Trump Accounts for newborns
top.description: The Treasury Department says a verification issue is blocking some 2026 babies with newly issued Social Security numbers from enrollment.

top.title: Le Pen says she will run for France’s presidency in 2027
top.description: A Paris appeals court upheld her embezzlement conviction but reduced her office ban, making her immediately eligible to run next April.

top.title: Lawmakers to press Bessent on Russia sanctions at NATO summit
top.description: A bipartisan congressional delegation plans to confront the Treasury secretary at a Tuesday dinner in Ankara over the stalled Sanctioning Russia Act.

top.title: Judge orders Trump to pay E. Jean Carroll $5.8M
top.description: A federal judge authorized the release of funds held in escrow, rejecting a last-minute filing by Trump's attorneys to block the payout.

top.title: Ukraine finds Monaco bombing suspect shot dead near Kyiv
top.description: A military intelligence officer confessed to killing Anastasiia Berezovska, the Interpol-wanted suspect in the June 29 attack on a Ukrainian tycoon.

top.title: US trade deficit jumps 42% in May on record imports
top.description: Capital goods imports hit a record $128 billion, driven by semiconductors and computer accessories tied to AI data center buildout.

top.title: Trump says he will remove Syria from terrorism list
top.description: The announcement came during a bilateral meeting with Syrian President al-Sharaa at the NATO summit in Ankara.

top.title: Belgium routs USMNT 4-1 to end US World Cup run
top.description: Charles De Ketelaere scored twice as Belgium eliminated the United States in the Round of 16 on Monday in Seattle.

top.title: CISA now using Anthropic's Mythos to audit government code
top.description: The deployment caps months of effort to get the powerful AI vulnerability-detection model into the hands of the lead U.S. civilian cybersecurity agency.

top.title: Trump declares Iran ceasefire 'over,' threatens to cut trade with Spain at NATO summit
top.description: As U.S. and Iranian forces traded strikes overnight, the president told allies in Ankara the preliminary truce was finished and singled out Spain for punishment.

top.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, offers power to UN-backed panel
top.description: Israel dismissed the move as "deception with no practical significance," saying Hamas members remained in their positions.

top.title: Preliminary hearing begins for man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
top.description: Prosecutors in Provo are presenting evidence against Tyler Robinson as Kirk's widow and parents face the defendant for the first time.

top.title: Hegseth cancels Israel visit as US-Iran strikes escalate
top.description: The defense secretary's trip was meant to address Israeli concerns over Trump's openness to selling F-35 jets to Turkey.

top.title: Iran's hardliners demand revenge as Khamenei funeral procession moves through Tehran
top.description: Tehran's airspace was shut down Monday as millions joined the procession and military commanders vowed to avenge the slain leader.

top.title: Three Khamenei sons appear at funeral; successor stays hidden
top.description: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei remains absent from his father's massive state funeral over fears Israel could assassinate him.

top.title: FEMA threatens to withhold terrorism funds over election rules
top.description: States must overhaul voting systems or lose 20% of homeland security grants under new conditions issued by the Trump administration.

top.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed at Madison Square Garden
top.description: Adam Sandler officiated as roughly 1,000 guests watched the couple marry Friday night in custom Dior at the iconic NYC arena.

top.title: White supremacist group Patriot Front marches through DC on America's 250th birthday
top.description: Hundreds of masked white nationalists carried Confederate flags and chanted anti-immigrant slogans near the Capitol on Saturday amid July 4th celebrations.

top.title: Housing bill set to become law as Trump's deadline to act expires
top.description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act will take effect without the president's signature after he refused to sign the landmark legislation.

top.title: US-Iran nuclear talks to resume July 11 in Pakistan
top.description: Negotiations paused for Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral will resume with sanctions, frozen assets, and Tehran's nuclear program on the agenda.

top.title: Messi scores 20th World Cup goal as Argentina edges Cape Verde 3-2 in extra time
top.description: Defending champions needed extra time to survive Cape Verde in a round of 32 thriller at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens.

top.title: NATO launches $40B counter-drone push at Ankara summit
top.description: Allies announced the Drone Edge initiative and tens of billions in arms deals as leaders seek to show Trump that defense pledges are becoming real capabilities.

top.title: Houthis threaten Saudi airports over Yemeni airspace dispute
top.description: The warning came after Saudi fighter jets allegedly tried to stop an Iranian civilian plane from landing at Sanaa airport.

top.title: Fired Joint Chiefs chair CQ Brown warns against domestic troop use
top.description: In a Foreign Affairs essay published ahead of July Fourth, Brown and co-authors say politically charged military missions erode combat readiness and public trust.

top.title: Trump says Greenland should be under US control at NATO summit
top.description: Speaking alongside Turkey's Erdogan in Ankara, the president said the territory "doesn't help Denmark" and should belong to America.

top.title: France pulls aircraft carrier from Gulf, citing US-Iran deal progress
top.description: Macron ordered the Charles de Gaulle back to Toulon but kept mine-clearance vessels and frigates near the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Brussels rejects UK bid for decision-making role in EU
top.description: The dispute over governance in post-Brexit trade talks has further delayed a reset deal already stalled by Labour's leadership transition.

top.title: Trump admin keeps same firm to repair Reflecting Pool liner
top.description: The Interior secretary confirmed on Sunday the administration will not seek new bids, keeping the original contractor despite ongoing scrutiny of no-bid deals.

top.title: June heatwave may have killed 20,000 in Europe, study estimates
top.description: Swiss glaciers hit their annual tipping point weeks early as researchers warn the historic late-June heat dome exposed $15.6 trillion in economic activity to climate-amplified conditions.

top.title: Russia's fuel crisis spreads as gasoline runs out in major port city
top.description: Novorossiysk reported zero gasoline at all stations on Thursday as Ukrainian drone strikes knock out a third of Russia's refining capacity.

top.title: Trump delivers late-night July 4 speech after storms delay America 250 celebration
top.description: Severe weather forced a two-hour evacuation of the National Mall, pushing the president's remarks past 11 p.m. on the nation's 250th birthday.

top.title: WHO declares cruise ship hantavirus outbreak over
top.description: The two-month outbreak aboard the MV Hondius infected 13 people, killed three, and triggered monitoring across 33 countries.

top.title: FBI orders 260 analysts to Atlanta for 2020 election probe
top.description: Director Kash Patel's memo directs all field offices to "immediately surge support" to investigate the 2020 vote in Fulton County, Georgia.

top.title: Trump pardons 11, including 9 for Clean Air Act violations
top.description: Nine of the 11 were convicted of disabling or bypassing vehicle emissions controls, which Trump called wrongful prosecution for "fixing their car."

top.title: Ex-Olympian indicted on felony charge over Reflecting Pool incident
top.description: David Hearn, who says he only touched a loose piece of liner during a bike ride, now faces up to 10 years in prison.

top.title: Security guard pulled alive 8 days after Venezuela quakes
top.description: Hernán Alberto Gil Flores was extracted from a collapsed shopping center basement in La Guaira after surviving in an air pocket since June 24.

top.title: Netanyahu, Trump agree to meet in US amid Iran deal tensions
top.description: The Friday phone call came as reports alleged Israel plotted to assassinate Iranian negotiators during U.S.-Tehran talks this spring.

top.title: Police fire tear gas as Albania 'Flamingo Revolution' protest turns violent
top.description: Clashes outside parliament in Tirana injured 12 officers and led to 18 detentions as "Flamingo Revolution" protesters demanded PM Rama's resignation.

top.title: Anthropic in talks with Samsung to build custom AI chip
top.description: The discussions focus on Samsung's 2nm process technology, though no detailed design or manufacturing work has begun, according to The Information.

top.title: Acting intel chief Pulte fires dozens more intelligence officials
top.description: A second wave of terminations targeted those the Trump administration considers "deep state," according to an intelligence official who spoke anonymously.

top.title: Iran rejects IAEA access to bombed nuclear sites after Doha talks
top.description: Parliament Speaker Qalibaf said inspectors are limited to two facilities, as indirect U.S.-Iran talks in Doha yielded working groups but no breakthrough.

top.title: US adds just 57,000 jobs in June, missing forecasts
top.description: The sharp slowdown from spring's strong hiring streak raises fresh questions about labor market health amid elevated inflation.

top.title: Trump returns to Mount Rushmore as White House backs adding his face
top.description: The president is delivering a keynote address Friday at the monument's first fireworks display in six years, kicking off America's 250th birthday weekend.

top.title: Vatican declares SSPX in schism, excommunicates all its clergy
top.description: The decree goes beyond the six bishops involved in Wednesday's unauthorized consecration, extending sanctions to all SSPX priests and adherent laity.

top.title: Driver charged with manslaughter in fatal Tesla crash into Texas home
top.description: Michael Butler faces manslaughter charges after his Tesla Model 3 crashed into a Katy, Texas, home on June 19, killing a 76-year-old woman inside.

top.title: Trump privately weighing clemency for Diddy Combs, CBS News reports
top.description: The president is meeting Friday to discuss pardons ahead of the Fourth of July, with Combs among those under consideration.

top.title: CSIS: Ukraine war casualties top 2 million since 2022
top.description: A new study estimates Russia has suffered 1.4 million casualties, while Ukraine's toll ranges between 525,000 and 625,000.

top.title: Musk denies report that SpaceX showed AI device to investors
top.description: The Wall Street Journal reported SpaceX presented a slim, AI-powered handset prototype to investors ahead of its IPO, but Musk called the claim "utterly false."

top.title: Trump says US destroyed Iran's radar in three nights of strikes
top.description: In a CNBC interview, Trump detailed how American forces hit Iranian radar systems and escorted oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Two masked climbers scale Empire State Building spire, unfurl peace banner
top.description: One climber appeared to propose to the other on a spire platform before the pair descended as NYPD helicopters hovered nearby.

top.title: Russia shuts rail border crossings with Finland, Estonia, Latvia
top.description: Moscow suspended all rail traffic through seven crossings effective Wednesday, offering no explanation or end date for the closures.

top.title: Hegseth's  Europe troop cuts blocked by Rubio ahead of NATO summit
top.description: The White House pulled back further force reductions after internal pushback, shifting focus to defense deals at next week's Ankara summit.

top.title: Meta building cloud business to sell excess AI compute
top.description: The social media giant plans to sell access to AI models and raw computing capacity, challenging AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.

top.title: Iran says nuclear talks won't advance until Lebanon commitments are met
top.description: Chief negotiator Ghalibaf called enrichment a "non-negotiable" red line as Netanyahu vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon indefinitely.

top.title: Trump made 21,000 stock trades in 2025, disclosure shows
top.description: The 927-page filing reveals hundreds of purchases the day before his tariff pause triggered a historic market rally.

top.title: US lifts export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 AI model
top.description: The Commerce Department's move completes the reversal of restrictions that forced Anthropic to take its most advanced AI models offline for nearly three weeks.

top.title: House GOP rebels tank defense bill vote over SAVE Act demands
top.description: Fourteen Republicans voted down a procedural rule for the $1.15 trillion NDAA, freezing the House floor days before July 4 recess.

top.title: Tucker Carlson vows to build new third party after breaking with Trump
top.description: Carlson told the Columbia Journalism Review he hasn't spoken to Trump since the Iran war began and called the U.S. "a one-party state."

top.title: USDA plans $500M aid for small meatpackers amid cattle shortage
top.description: The funding excludes the four largest beef processors and aims to keep smaller slaughterhouses operating as losses mount to $300 per head.

top.title: Supreme Court agrees to decide if AR-15 bans violate Second Amendment
top.description: The justices granted review in cases from Illinois and Connecticut, setting up the most significant gun rights showdown since the 2022 Bruen decision.

top.title: OpenAI proposes giving U.S. government a 5% stake, FT reports
top.description: The offer, worth roughly $42.6 billion, is the most concrete step yet in months of talks between Sam Altman and Trump administration officials.

top.title: Ukraine strikes Moscow satellite facility in 419-drone barrage
top.description: Zelensky confirmed drones hit Russia's Dubna Space Communications Center again as a six-month-old baby was killed in the Moscow region.

top.title: Rep. Tom Kean Jr. returns to Congress, reveals depression diagnosis
top.description: The New Jersey Republican disclosed that depression was behind his nearly four-month absence, during which he missed more than 100 votes.

top.title: US in advanced talks with AI firms on voluntary model release standards
top.description: The discussions aim to operationalize Trump's June executive order, which created a framework for pre-release government review of frontier AI models.

top.title: Supreme Court strikes down campaign spending limits
top.description: The 6-3 ruling removes 1974 caps on coordinated spending between political parties and candidates ahead of the November midterms.

top.title: UK signals intervention in Paramount's $110B Warner Bros. deal
top.description: Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy said she is "minded to intervene" in the proposed merger, citing concerns about media plurality for British audiences.

top.title: Trump reports $1.4B in crypto income as investors lost billions
top.description: His 927-page financial disclosure shows total 2025 income of at least $2.2 billion, more than triple what he reported the year before.

top.title: Supreme Court upholds state bans on transgender athletes
top.description: The 6-3 ruling rejected equal protection and Title IX challenges, backing laws in West Virginia and Idaho that restrict team participation by biological sex.

top.title: Russian recruits surviving only minutes at Ukraine's drone-dominated front
top.description: New soldiers last 20 to 35 minutes in combat positions as drone warfare drives losses past 30,000 per month, according to a Foreign Policy essay.

top.title: Trump renews vow to block China from Panama Canal at Roosevelt library dedication
top.description: Speaking at the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library opening in North Dakota, Trump reprised his second-term push to counter Chinese influence over the waterway.

top.title: Venezuela accused of blocking aid, rescuers after quakes kill over 1,700
top.description: Critics say government restrictions on access to the hardest-hit areas are hindering rescue operations five days after twin earthquakes struck.

top.title: San Francisco Archdiocese reaches $395M abuse settlement
top.description: The agreement resolves more than 500 clergy sexual abuse claims filed under California's AB 218 and establishes a trust for survivors.

top.title: Iran deal strains U.S.-Saudi ties as MBS hedges on Trump's diplomacy
top.description: Gulf allies fear the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding grants Tehran dangerous leverage over the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Europe heatwave kills over 1,300 as Paris morgues run out of space
top.description: France alone has recorded 1,000 excess deaths since the heatwave began, overwhelming mortuaries and sparking political criticism of the government's response.

top.title: JetBlue plane hits drone at 3,000 feet on approach to JFK
top.description: The aircraft landed safely with no damage found, but the incident marks the second drone encounter near a New York-area airport in days.

top.title: Trump takes first flight on Qatar-gifted Air Force One
top.description: The president flew the converted Boeing 747-8 to North Dakota for the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library dedication.

top.title: Supreme Court upholds state laws counting mail ballots after Election Day
top.description: The 5-4 ruling in Watson v. RNC preserves grace periods in roughly 30 states ahead of the 2026 midterms.

top.title: Comcast to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into separate company
top.description: Shares surged as the company said it will split its media and entertainment assets from its broadband and technology operations within a year.

top.title: Trump administration declines to extend USMCA trade pact
top.description: The decision triggers annual reviews and a 10-year countdown that could see the North American free trade zone expire in 2036.

top.title: Skydiving plane crash in France kills all 11 on board
top.description: A Pilatus PC-6 carrying five skydiving students, five instructors, and a pilot crashed shortly after takeoff near Nancy on Sunday morning.

top.title: South Korea scrambles jets as China, Russia hold air patrol
top.description: Nearly 10 Chinese and Russian military aircraft briefly entered South Korea's air defense zone during Saturday's 11th joint strategic patrol.

top.title: DOJ sues Virginia over assault weapons ban on day law takes effect
top.description: The federal lawsuit challenges the state's ban on AR-15-style rifles as unconstitutional, extending the Trump administration's campaign against state gun laws.

top.title: China strips generals, ex-regulator of lawmaker posts
top.description: Xi Jinping's anti-corruption drive expands as the NPC removes eight senior officials, including six PLA generals and a Politburo member.

top.title: Drone strikes Iranian Kurdish opposition camp near Erbil
top.description: No casualties were reported after the camp was evacuated before Saturday's attack, the latest in a sustained campaign throughout 2026.

top.title: Fed Chair Warsh vows to hit 2% inflation, rebuffs Trump on rates
top.description: Speaking at the ECB's Sintra forum, Warsh said the Fed will remain independent and dismissed expectations of tolerating above-target inflation.

top.title: Cape Verde becomes smallest nation to reach World Cup knockout round
top.description: The tournament debutants finished second in Group H with three draws and will face defending champions Argentina on July 3 in Miami.

top.title: Supreme Court calls Alito-Sotomayor clash a 'misunderstanding'
top.description: The court said Friday that Alito was notified in advance of Sotomayor's oral dissent in a major asylum case but misunderstood the situation.

top.title: Trump announces first-ever GOP midterm convention in Dallas
top.description: The unprecedented Sept. 9-10 gathering aims to rally Republican voters ahead of competitive House and Senate races this November.

top.title: CDC raises Ebola response to highest level amid DRC outbreak
top.description: The Level 1 activation, the most severe designation, deploys maximum staff as the Bundibugyo strain spreads across central and east Africa.

top.title: Texas approves Bible readings for 5M public school students
top.description: The State Board of Education voted Friday to make Texas the first state to require Bible passages in its public school reading curriculum.

top.title: Trump demands gas retailers cut prices to $2.50, warns of 'big problems'
top.description: The president escalated his pressure campaign Monday, threatening retailers who don't lower pump prices despite a DOJ probe already targeting major oil companies.

top.title: Israel, Lebanon reach framework deal after extended Washington talks
top.description: The agreement establishes a mechanism for Lebanese army units to deploy into pilot zones vacated by Israeli forces in southern Lebanon.

top.title: Mistrial declared in Palisades Fire arson trial
top.description: Jurors voted 10-2 for acquittal but could not reach unanimity, forcing prosecutors to decide whether to retry the case.

top.title: Supreme Court overturns 91-year-old limit on presidential firing power
top.description: The 6-3 ruling in Trump v. Slaughter lets presidents dismiss leaders of independent agencies without cause, reshaping federal regulation.

top.title: Newsom calls for national billionaires' tax while opposing California's version
top.description: The governor proposed a minimum tax on those worth over $100 million and a public AI equity fund as he eyes a 2028 presidential run.

top.title: Iran strikes caused $400M in damage to US Navy base, WSJ finds
top.description: The Pentagon is now weighing relocating some Gulf installations to Israel and moving command centers underground, according to the investigation.

top.title: Putin rejects Ukraine's ceasefire proposal, vows to seize four regions
top.description: Ukraine's foreign minister declared the "Spirit of Anchorage" between Trump and Putin "certainly dead," urging Russia to negotiate directly.

top.title: SpaceX plans Starlink mobile service to rival U.S. wireless carriers
top.description: Gwynne Shotwell told IPO roadshow investors that SpaceX could sell mobile contracts directly to consumers and build its own terrestrial network.

top.title: NYC approves historic rent freeze on stabilized apartments
top.description: The Rent Guidelines Board voted Thursday to freeze rents on roughly one million apartments, delivering on Mayor Mamdani's central campaign promise.

top.title: Trump declares reflecting pool 'in full use,' eyes D.C. golf course
top.description: The president said construction on a public golf course near the National Mall will begin in September as part of his broader reshaping of the capital.

top.title: NY prosecutors drop Weinstein rape charge after accuser declines to testify again
top.description: Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's office dismissed the third-degree rape charge after Jessica Mann said she "can no longer go through this."

top.title: US tests Ukrainian Magura naval drones in Philippines
top.description: The combat-proven Magura V5 sank a target ship during Balikatan 2026, marking its first use outside the Russia-Ukraine war.

top.title: Trump sons invested in Kazakhstan deal their father brokered, NYT finds
top.description: A New York Times investigation reveals Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump took a stake in a $1.6 billion taxpayer-backed tungsten project weeks after the president helped negotiate it.

top.title: Supreme Court rules federal law blocks Roundup cancer lawsuits
top.description: The decision shields **Bayer** from thousands of claims alleging the company should have labeled the herbicide as a potential carcinogen.

top.title: Macron says Europe and US in 'reconvergence' on Ukraine
top.description: Europe's five biggest military powers met in Berlin on Wednesday to coordinate ahead of next month's NATO summit in Ankara.

top.title: Trump taps Oklahoma trooper as ICE director nominee
top.description: If confirmed, Lance Schroyer would be the first Senate-confirmed ICE director since 2017, ending nearly a decade of acting leaders.

top.title: 6.9 magnitude earthquake strikes off northeastern Japan
top.description: The quake hit near Kuji, Iwate Prefecture, on Thursday morning local time, prompting a tsunami warning for the region.

top.title: ICC bureau recommends firing prosecutor Karim Khan
top.description: Details of the June 8 decision emerged Tuesday, revealing findings of "non-consensual sexual contact" with a junior staff member.

top.title: Trump threatens 100% tariff on countries with digital services taxes
top.description: The sweeping threat, posted Friday on Truth Social, would override existing trade deals and target European nations considering taxes on U.S. tech giants.

top.title: Rubio says U.S.-Iran talks to resume in Switzerland June 29
top.description: The secretary of state, on a Gulf tour to reassure allies, also rejected any Iranian tolls on the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Venezuela set to reveal $240B debt in largest restructuring
top.description: The figure far exceeds prior market estimates of $150 billion to $200 billion, according to the Financial Times.

top.title: UN demands probes into 52 ICE custody deaths under Trump
top.description: UN rights chief Volker Türk called for independent investigations as a new report found the ICE detention mortality rate has more than doubled.

top.title: OpenAI unveils Jalapeño, its first custom AI chip
top.description: Developed with Broadcom, the inference chip rivals Nvidia's Blackwell processors, according to Broadcom CEO Hock Tan.

top.title: Ex-Adams chief of staff Frank Carone arrested by FBI
top.description: Carone and three others face a federal bribery case tied to a city contract issued during the Adams administration.

top.title: Trump admin seeks $1.4B for Ebola as Kenya facility faces scrutiny
top.description: Public Health Service members describe rushed training for the controversial Kenya quarantine center as the outbreak nears 1,000 cases.

top.title: China defends right to target people abroad under new ethnic unity law
top.description: Vice Justice Minister Hu Weilie dismissed "long-arm jurisdiction" criticism, saying the law taking effect July 1 is "legitimate and lawful."

top.title: US warns 'time is not on Moscow's side' as peace talks stall
top.description: Putin and Lavrov insisted on Moscow's terms for Ukraine negotiations as the U.S. called for an immediate ceasefire at the UN Security Council.

top.title: Judge lets lawsuit against Trump's $1.8B fund proceed, questions DOJ's 'trustworthiness'
top.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday the DOJ's refusal to submit a sworn statement confirming the fund's closure keeps the legal challenge alive.

top.title: Mamdani-backed candidates sweep three NYC congressional primaries
top.description: The New York mayor's progressive slate toppled incumbents and establishment Democrats in Tuesday's primary races across the city.

top.title: Kim commissions North Korea's largest warship, pledges nuclear navy
top.description: The 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon entered service Tuesday after 14 months of testing, as Kim outlined plans for 10,000-ton warships.

top.title: Supreme Court rules Trump can end TPS for Haitians, Syrians
top.description: The 6-3 decision strips protections from over 350,000 immigrants and could affect up to 1.3 million TPS holders nationwide.

top.title: Alan Wilson wins South Carolina GOP governor runoff
top.description: Wilson defeated Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette by a wide margin after Trump hedged his endorsement by backing both candidates.

top.title: Meta is building a prediction markets app to rival Polymarket and Kalshi
top.description: CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed a team to develop "Arena," a standalone app that would initially use a points system instead of real money.

top.title: Supreme Court lets Trump turn back asylum seekers at border
top.description: The 6-3 ruling in Mullin v. Al Otro Lado holds that migrants standing in Mexico have not "arrived" in the U.S. under federal law.

top.title: Bennett says Israel smuggled Starlink into Iran
top.description: Former PM told a Jerusalem summit he initiated covert delivery of tens of thousands of satellite receivers to help Iranian protesters.

top.title: DOJ charges 455 in $6.5 billion healthcare fraud takedown
top.description: The annual crackdown includes a record number of Medicaid fraud defendants and targets schemes involving false billing, opioid distribution, and wound care fraud.

top.title: Senate reverses Iran war powers vote after Trump confrontation
top.description: Two Republican senators changed their positions late Wednesday after a shouting match between Trump and GOP lawmakers at the Capitol.

top.title: DOJ, New York file dueling lawsuits over mask ban for federal agents
top.description: New York's law barring officers from wearing face coverings takes effect Friday, setting up an urgent legal clash over immigration enforcement.

top.title: US, Iran finalize deal to release $12B in frozen assets
top.description: Iran's chief negotiator says the agreement, reached in Switzerland, will release the funds in two $6 billion tranches.

top.title: Trump orders DOJ to probe Exxon, Chevron over gas prices
top.description: The president accused major oil companies of "gouging" consumers by keeping pump prices high despite falling crude costs after the Iran peace deal.
